name: dnstwist
title: dnstwist
description:
Domain name permutation engine
dnstwist generates a list of similarly looking domain names for
a given domain name and performs DNS queries for them (A, AAAA,
NS and MX). For MX records it checks whether there is an active mail
server which could be used to intercept misdirected emails.
Additionally it estimates webpage similarity based on fuzzy hashes.
This functionality might be helpful in detecting typosquatters, phishing
attacks, fraud and corporate espionage.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use dnstwist because Domain name permutation engine.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use dnstwist as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `dnstwist [OPTION...] DOMAIN` as documented upstream for dnstwist.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair dnstwist with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `dnstwist --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install dnstwist. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `dnstwist [OPTION...] DOMAIN`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: Domain name permutation engine.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: dnstwist - domain name permutation engine.
